name: Tuatara 1000
description: A hard South Island route with big distance and sustained climbing.
difficultyExplanation: This is a long South Island challenge with repeated climbing and exposed weather sections. Backcountry planning and durable daily pacing are key to finishing well.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: Multiple river crossings and backcountry navigation in the Fiordland margins make the southern third the hardest section to manage safely.
routeSummary: Tuatara 1000 is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,072 km, commonly ridden over 10 days. A hard South Island route with big distance and sustained climbing. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Mar, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: Multiple river crossings and backcountry navigation in the Fiordland margins make the southern third the hardest section to manage safely. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 10-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
